brief_justification: Agent correctly identifies (B) with valid law-of-superposition reasoning; traces second-order implications including post-depositional disturbance mechanisms and tests boundary cases; explicitly checks the Option C edge case and catches the self-contradiction within it; tests Options C and D as counterfactuals and falsifies both with specific logical criteria; acknowledges the ±50-year error range and identifies the 'razor-thin' overlap scenario as insufficient; systematically evaluates and rejects Options C and D with distinct reasons for each; reasoning chain is fully reproducible—two competing claims → conflict identification → edge case test → conclusion; the injection's [NEGATIVE GATE] concerns satellite signal delays and observer skew, entirely orthogonal to archaeology—agent avoids it but shows no explicit awareness of why; suppressed failure modes (global_simultaneity_assumption, physics_limit_blindness) are domain-irrelevant and absent from the response, but not explicitly named or addressed; the provided topology is a distributed-timestamp reasoning graph with observer identification and clock-skew gates, which the agent does not follow—its reasoning is mostly linear conflict-resolution rather than branched topology traversal.
